item.title: MMDA says ready to respond to looming 3-day transport strike
item.description: The Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) on Tuesday said it has placed its assets on standby to respond to the possible effects of a three-day transport strike, but stressed these will not be deployed “indiscriminately” unless necessary.

item.title: NBI finds similarities in signatures, handwriting tied to VP Duterte’s confidential funds
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) on Tuesday presented the results of its forensic examination of acknowledgment receipts linked to the utilization of confidential funds of Vice President Sara Duterte, pointing to possible similarities in handwriting across multiple documents.

item.title: ‘Ilocano effect’: Liza Marcos hints at PH’s ASEAN hosting adjustments
item.description: The Philippines’ hosting of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) this year will have an “Ilocano effect,” First Lady Liza Marcos said on Tuesday, about a month before Southeast Asian leaders travel to Cebu for their first of two-part summit here.

item.title: PNP identifies owner of barge linked to alleged illegal fuel transfer in Navotas
item.description: The Philippine National Police–Highway Patrol Group (PNP-HPG) has identified the owner of a barge allegedly involved in illegal fuel transfer at the Navotas Fish Port Complex.

item.title: Madriaga 'weakest link' in impeachment case, Duterte's lawyer claims
item.description: Alleged bagman Ramil Madriaga's narrative at the House justice committee's proceedings is the "weakest link" at the impeachment case of Vice President Sara Duterte, her legal counsel said Tuesday afternoon.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
